clinical_criteria: Exclude samples collected during inpatient hospitalization (admit date ≤ collection date ≤ discharge date) unless it was the only sample available
Exclude if hematologic disorders or solid-organ malignancies were active 2 years before or 5 years after sample collection
Exclude if chemotherapy occurred in 4th or 5th year after mention of active hematologic disorder/malignancy
Exclude if bone marrow/solid-organ transplantation occurred anytime before or 3 years after sample collection
Exclude if chronic liver disease/cirrhosis diagnosed on or before sample collection date
Exclude if hereditary anemias ever diagnosed before or after sample collection
Exclude if specific medications used 2 months before or 2 months after sample collection
Exclude if major surgery or trauma occurred 3 months before sample collection
algorithm_summary: This phenotype defines red blood cell indices (hemoglobin, MCV, MCH, RDW, etc.) and ESR measurements from laboratory databases. The algorithm uses a multi-step exclusion approach to identify valid outpatient samples by excluding patients with: 1) Active hematologic disorders or malignancies within specified timeframes, 2) History of transplantation, 3) Chronic liver disease/cirrhosis, 4) Hereditary anemias, 5) Use of specific medications that could affect RBC indices, and 6) Recent major surgery or trauma. The validation form provides a systematic checklist to determine patient eligibility based on these exclusion criteria, with specific time windows for each exclusion category.
